questions.scenario: A ticket says `fix the orders bucket`. There are receipt, export, and failed-payload buckets with similar names. What should the ticket include before approval?

options.text: Project, bucket name, location, labels, relevant object prefix, and the evidence that points to this bucket.
Only the word `orders`, because search results are enough.
A new random bucket name to avoid investigating.

questions.explanation: A good evidence bundle lets another engineer find the same resource and understand why it is the target.

questions.scenario: A Cloud Run service cannot read a secret. Which evidence best connects the resource and the caller?

options.text: The Cloud Run service's project, region, runtime service account, secret resource, and IAM evidence.
The billing account display name only.
A screenshot of a different project where a secret exists.

questions.explanation: Secret access depends on the exact service, runtime identity, secret, project, and IAM binding. Loose evidence can point at the wrong target.

questions.scenario: Which items belong in evidence before a production resource change?

options.text: Project ID and resource path or exact name.
Labels that show service, environment, team, and component.
A vague memory that the resource looked familiar last week.

questions.explanation: Project, resource identity, location, and labels make a change reviewable. Memory is not production evidence.
